title: [bug] iterate-stream causes race conditions in streaming mode
author: yocontra
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When using async iterables to parse a workbook as a stream, randomly the zip parser hangs due to [utils/iterate-stream](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/lib/utils/iterate-stream.js) specifically [here](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/lib/stream/xlsx/workbook-reader.js#L88).

Just from a quick glance the utility is really non-standard and looks error prone.

Lib version: 4.2.0

## Steps To Reproduce

This is extremely difficult to reproduce and I haven't been able to make an isolated test case. I have been loading up 100 parsers concurrently in a loop to reproduce this specific race condition.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

This utility isn't needed since all readable streams are async iterable in node core already, this is probably just old code from before this feature landed in core. Simple removing usage of this utility and just using the streams as iterables directly solved the bug. If this all seems sound to you, I can send a PR to remove the utility and switch to the core behavior. I noticed some other behavior that should be cleaned up around temp file management as well that I can address in the same PR.

comments.author: yocontra
comments.body: Node core implementation of what iterate-stream is doing for reference: https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/master/lib/internal/streams/readable.js#L1047-L1126

comments.author: BigKamil5
comments.body:
Is there any progress in this case? Form me problem occurs in the `WorkbookReader`. Not every zip stream entry get into the content because of the race condition. 
And because of that... some of the parse functions are not called. For me its `_parseSharedStrings`

Seems like removing iterate-stream is helping
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/37780630/daf2cda1-bc89-4bf9-9a93-58d47e508d7d)

Can we merge it sometime? Or at least check... if `iterate-stream` can be ommited
